front: Opera: Puccini's final unfinished opera, completed by Franco Alfano
Opera: premiere conductor reportedly stopped at the point where the composer's manuscript ended, saying 'at this point the maestro died'
Opera: Prince Calaf wins a contest of three riddles to claim the title princess's hand in marriage
Opera: Calaf then issues a Rumpelstiltskin-like counter-challenge — Turandot must discover his name before dawn or release him
Opera: incorporates the Chinese folk melody 'Mo Li Hua' (Jasmine Flower), which Puccini heard on a music box
Opera: Luciano Berio composed an alternative ending in 2002 to correct dramaturgical flaws in the standard completion
Opera: servants Ping, Pang, and Pong torture slave girl Liu to learn a man's name; a gong is struck three times
Opera: based on a play of the same name by Carlo Gozzi
Opera: tenor aria ending with three cries of 'Vincerò!' on a sustained A4; translated as 'Let no one sleep'
Opera: tenor aria beginning in G major on D4 in a double-dotted 'LONG, short, LONG, LONG' rhythm, repeated an octave below
Opera: staple of Luciano Pavarotti's repertoire; performed at multiple FIFA World Cup finals
Opera: in 2020, tenor Maurizio Marchini sang this aria from a Florentine balcony during the Italian COVID lockdown, going viral
Opera: performance tradition holds the penultimate high B much longer than the semiquaver notated in the score
Opera: Act 2 soprano aria in which Turandot explains the rape and murder of her ancestor Princess Lo-u-Ling to justify her hatred of men
Opera: soprano aria with ostinato alternating between F-sharp sus2 sus4 and F-sharp minor add9 chords; repeats 'No one will ever possess me!'
Opera: title translates as 'In this palace'
Opera: aria in which slave girl Liu proclaims the title princess to be 'encircled in ice' before stabbing herself
back: "Tu che di gel sei cinta" from Turandot (Puccini)

front: Opera: aria whose text was written by the composer himself rather than the librettist
back: "Tu che di gel sei cinta" from Turandot (Puccini)
